title: Upon A Comely And Curious Maid.
content:
If men can say that beauty dies,
    Marbles will swear that here it lies.
    If, reader, then thou canst forbear
    In public loss to shed a tear,
    The dew of grief upon this stone
    Will tell thee pity thou hast none.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon A Comely And Curious Maid."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
